guide.title: Skill Development Guide
guide.subtitle: Learn how to create spm-compliant skill packages

structure.title: Skill Directory Structure
structure.desc: A skill is a directory containing SKILL.md as the core entry point that defines metadata and AI instructions. The directory can also include templates, scripts, references, and other supporting files — all of which are deployed together during installation.
structure.tree:
my-skill/
├── SKILL.md          # Core: metadata + AI instructions (required)
├── templates/        # Template files (optional)
│   └── report.md
├── scripts/          # Helper scripts (optional)
│   └── setup.sh
├── references/       # Reference materials (optional)
│   └── api-spec.json
└── examples/         # Example files (optional)
    └── sample-output.md

frontmatter.title: Frontmatter Fields
frontmatter.desc: Every SKILL.md begins with YAML frontmatter declaring metadata, trigger conditions, and permission requirements.

rows.name: Skill name — lowercase alphanumeric + hyphens, must start with letter or digit
rows.author: Author name

trigger.title: Trigger Configuration (trigger)
trigger.desc: Controls when the AI model selects this skill. Required for publishable skills.
trigger.triggerDesc: Natural language description read by the AI model to decide when to use this skill
trigger.tags: Keyword tag array for skill discovery — minimum 1 tag
trigger.filePatterns: Glob patterns for file-based matching, optional
trigger.priority: Priority (0–100, default 50). 0–19: demos; 20–39: general community; 40–59: specialized (default); 60–79: domain-specific; 80–100: core/system

security.title: Security Permissions (security.permissions)
security.desc: Declare runtime permissions. List only granted permissions as a flat string array.

dependencies.title: Dependencies & Compatibility
dependencies.desc: Declare skill dependencies and runtime compatibility requirements.
dependencies.deps: Depend on other skills using author/name: semver-range format
dependencies.compatibility: Compatibility: min_context_tokens, requires (capabilities), models (restrict to models, empty = all)

body.title: Body Writing Guide
body.desc: The SKILL.md body uses XML tags following Anthropic's prompt engineering best practices. Tags create semantic boundaries for reliable model parsing.

tags.context: Define expertise scope, skill purpose, and why it exists
tags.examples: Input/output demonstration cases (2–3 examples significantly improve output quality)
tags.guidelines: Constraints with motivation for better generalization
tags.custom: Custom tags — use semantic names like <stack>, <patterns>, <avoid>

tips.title: Writing Tips
tips.desc: Key principles for writing high-quality skills.
tips.specific: Be specific — name exact libraries, APIs, file formats. The model follows literally.
tips.positive: Positive framing — "Always validate inputs" works better than "Don't skip validation".
tips.motivation: Include motivation — "Use pooling (keeps resources predictable)" helps the model generalize.
tips.examples: Add examples — cover a typical case and an edge case.
tips.concise: Keep concise — every token counts against the context budget.

example.title: Complete Example
example.desc: A complete SKILL.md template with all key sections.
example.code:
---
name: report-generator
version: 1.0.0
author: your-name
license: MIT
description: "Generate structured reports from data files"
language: en

trigger:
  description: "Use when creating reports from CSV, JSON, or database data"
  tags: [report, data, analysis, csv]
  file_patterns: ["*.csv", "*.json"]
  priority: 50

security:
  permissions: [file:read, file:write]

compatibility:
  min_context_tokens: 3000
  requires: [file_system]
  models: []
---

<context>
Expert in data analysis and report generation. This skill solves
the challenge of producing professional reports from various data
sources, providing data cleaning, statistical analysis, and
visualization recommendations.
</context>

<instructions>
1. Read and parse user-provided data files (CSV, JSON)
2. Perform data cleaning: handle missing values, outliers, format normalization
3. Run statistical analysis: distributions, trends, correlations
4. Generate a structured report with summary, chart suggestions, and conclusions
</instructions>

<examples>
<example>
<input>User uploads a sales CSV dataset</input>
<output>Generates a report with monthly trends, top 10 products, and regional breakdown</output>
</example>
<example>
<input>User provides JSON-formatted API response data</input>
<output>Extracts key fields and generates an API health analysis report</output>
</example>
</examples>

<guidelines>
- Always validate data format and completeness first (avoids wasting time on dirty data)
- Attach confidence levels and sample sizes to statistics (helps user assess reliability)
- Note recommended chart types with reasoning for each visualization
</guidelines>

<verification>
- [ ] Data successfully parsed with no format errors
- [ ] Missing values and outliers properly handled
- [ ] Report contains summary, analysis, and conclusion sections
- [ ] All values include units and context
</verification>

validate.title: Validation & Installation
validate.desc: After development, follow these steps to validate and use your skill in SPM Manager.
validate.validateDesc: Ensure frontmatter contains all required fields (name, version, author, license, description), name matches ^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9-]*$ format, and version is valid semver.
validate.linkDesc: Commit the skill directory to a configured remote Git repository. SPM Manager supports two repository structures:
validate.publishDesc: Click "Sync Remote" in SPM Manager to fetch the latest repository, then install the skill from the Global or Project page. Installation copies the entire skill directory to the Agent's skill path (e.g. .claude/skills/).
validate.manifestDesc: Place a skills.json file at the repository root to explicitly declare all skills with their names, paths, and metadata.
validate.manifestTree:
my-repo/
├── skills.json       # Manifest file (highest priority)
├── skill-a/
│   └── SKILL.md
└── skill-b/
    └── SKILL.md
validate.scanDesc: Without skills.json, SPM Manager auto-scans subdirectories containing SKILL.md. It scans both direct subdirectories at the repo root and direct subdirectories under skills/.
validate.scanTree:
my-repo/
├── skill-a/           # Skill at repo root
│   └── SKILL.md
└── skills/             # Skills under skills/ subdirectory
    ├── skill-b/
    │   └── SKILL.md
    └── skill-c/
        └── SKILL.md
